item.title: Pakistan ready to host US-Iran peace talks
item.description: Pakistan was poised on Friday to host Iranian and US delegations for negotiations in its capital, although Tehran’s participation remained uncertain after deadly Israeli strikes on Lebanon threatened this week’s temporary truce......

item.title: 20 key ords lose validity
item.description: Twenty ordinances promulgated by the interim government, including key reform measures on judicial independence, human rights, referendum, enforced disappearance prevention and anti-corruption, are losing their validity from today as they failed to secure approval from Jatiya Sangsad within the constitutionally mandated timeframe......

item.title: Multifaceted pressure strain consumers
item.description: Consumers have been facing multifaceted pressure at kitchen markets as prices of essential food items remain elevated and supply disruptions persist......

item.title: RMG exports to major markets fall in July-Mar
item.description: The country’s readymade garment exports to major global destinations declined persistently during July-March period of the current financial year 2025-26 due to weak global demand......

item.title: Parliament passes Deposit Protection Bill, 2026
item.description: The ‘Deposit Protection Bill, 2026’ was passed in Parliament on Friday to safeguard the interests of depositors and ensure stability in the country’s financial sector......

item.title: Dhaka shops report drop in sweetmeat sales
item.description: Sweetmeat shops across the capital are experiencing a decline in sales as economic sluggishness and ripple effects of the ongoing Middle East conflict impact consumer spending......

item.title: Gas crisis flagged as major challenge for Rajshahi industries
item.description: Adviser to the prime minister Rashed Al Mahmud Titumir on Friday called for giving importance to both industrialisation and agro-based development to utilise the vast potential of the Rajshahi region......

item.title: City Bank posts Tk 1,324cr profit in 2025
item.description: City Bank PLC has reported posted a consolidated net profit of Tk 1,324 crore in 2025, registering 31 per cent growth from Tk 1,014 crore in the previous year......

item.title: Russian envoy in US for economy talks: Kremlin
item.description: Kremlin envoy Kirill Dmitriev is in the United States for talks on the economy, Russia said Friday, stressing that he would not be holding discussions on the Ukraine war......

item.title: Shipping toll for Hormuz passage divides nations
item.description: Iran’s imposition of a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz looks set to be a key point of contention at talks opening in Pakistan Friday......

item.title: IMF urges nations to ‘do no harm’ in fiscal response to Iran war
item.description: The International Monetary Fund’s chief urged governments on Thursday to ‘do no harm’ as they face the massive economic shocks caused by the Middle East war, calling for ‘targeted’ and disciplined fiscal actions......

item.title: Pre-war data shows weaker US growth, drop in income
item.description: Government data released Thursday painted a lacklustre picture of the US economy prior to the Middle East war, downgrading fourth-quarter growth and showing an unexpected decline in US personal income in February......

item.title: Just 10 vessels pass thru Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire
item.description: Just 10 vessels have passed through Strait of Hormuz since the Middle East war ceasefire took effect, according to maritime tracking data, amid lingering fears about using the strategic waterway......

item.title: Global stocks climb, oil steadies on guarded optimism over Iran war ceasefire
item.description: Stock markets rose and oil prices flattened Friday with investors nervously optimistic about the shaky US-Iran ceasefire holding ahead of weekend talks aimed at finding a long-lasting truce......

item.title: IMF to cut global growth forecast due to Mideast war
item.description: The International Monetary Fund will lower global growth forecasts due to the Middle East war, its chief said Thursday, warning of the conflict’s ‘scarring effects’ despite a fragile ceasefire......

item.title: Nigeria’s in vogue handwoven fabric makers resist automation
item.description: In Iseyin, a sleepy town in southwestern Nigeria, shaded spaces under trees, makeshift sheds and narrow alleys double as production hubs for aso-oke, a handwoven fabric indigenous to the Yoruba ethnic group......

item.title: Ceasefire or pause: Gulf held hostage
item.description: THE ceasefire offered a much-needed breather for the Arab Gulf states. Donald Trump’s threat to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure could have unleashed a human catastrophe across the region with the potential to spiral into an uncontrollable conflagration......

item.title: Understanding Baishakhi economy
item.description: PAHELA Baishakh, the Bengali new year, is celebrated with enthusiasm quite uinmatched. Streets flood with processions, music fills public squares and markets bustle with shoppers seeking traditional food, dresses and gifts. While the festival is widely recognised for its cultural significance, it also serves as a powerful driver of economic activities, especially......

item.title: Who benefits from economic growth?
item.description: BANGLADESH’S development has depended on export-led growth, particularly in the RMG sector, remittance inflows from migrant workers, contributions from service industries, and agricultural productivity. These areas have been crucial in creating jobs and earning foreign currency. Millions have been lifted out of extreme poverty and a growing middle class has......

item.title: Bangladesh in changing global order
item.description: IRONICALLY, the post-Cold War world that emerged after the fall of communism in 1991 is no longer the world we inhabit in 2026. Power Transition Theory (PTT), popularised by AF Organski, anticipated such shifts in the international system. Scholars such as Robert Gilpin further associated power transitions with the rise of a dominant hegemon capable......

item.title: Authorities must rein in edible oil cartels
item.description: A SUDDEN increase in prices of edible oil points to market manipulation by business cartels. Against the set prices, Tk 176 a litre for loose and Tk 195 for bottled soya bean oil, retailers continue to sell for inflated rates, with loose oil for as high as Tk 195 and bottled oil for as high as Tk 210. There is also a shortage of one- and two-litre bottles. Similar discrepancies persist......

item.title: A likely futile exercise
item.description: THE government has finally decided to introduce online classes three days a week and hold in-person classes for three other days, by curtailing the two-day weekend to a one-day affair in a select group of educational institutions only in the capital city in the next week. Online classes would take place Sundays, Tuesdays and Thursdays whilst in-person classes would take place......

item.title: Young women politicians face growing barriers: workshop
item.description: Speakers at a workshop on Friday stated that the country’s young women politicians were increasingly facing social, family andpoliticalbarriers......Politics

item.description: The police on Friday detained a man over the allegations of cutting off a child’s penis during circumcision in Dhaka Udyan area in the capital......

item.description: Husband of a Jatiyatabadi Mohila Dal leader has allegedly illegally occupied the office of Kalapara upazila Awami League in Patuakhali, turning it to a storeroom......

item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh has intensified operational and intelligence activities across the country, including border areas for keeping the edible oil market stable......

item.description: The police on Friday detained the main accused of an illegal meat-selling ring, in connection with the recovery of several slaughtered horses and a live horse from an abandoned house at Gazaria of Munshiganj......Geographic Reference

item.description: A MOBILE court in Sirajganj has sentenced two youths to two months of imprisonment for hoarding and selling fuel oil in the Solonga Sahebganj area of Ullapara upazila......

item.description: A rickshaw-van puller was allegedly beaten to death by his neighbour over a trivial matter at Belgachhi village in Mohonpur upazila of Rajshahi on Friday......Bangladesh travel

item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Friday warned of tough action against those involved in question paper leaks, saying no one would be spared in ensuring fair examinations across the country......

item.title: Thousands denied entry since EU’s automated border system rollout
item.description: Thousands of people were refused entry to the EU and almost 700 identified as ‘a security threat’ since the phasing-in of a new border check system that became fully operational Friday, Brussels said......

item.description: At least 10 people were killed on Friday when a boat carrying dozens of passengers in northern India hit a pontoon bridge and capsized, police said......

item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky has said that Ukrainian military experts downed Iranian drones in several Middle East countries......

item.title: Diesel crisis disrupts irrigation, boro yield in Chuadanga
item.description: A SEVERE shortage of diesel in Jibannagar upazila of Chuadanga has disrupted irrigation during the peak Boro rice season, raising concerns among farmers over potential yield losses......

item.title: 2 arrested over fraud case
item.description: A Saudi expatriate’s mother and sister have been arrested at Shibaloy Upazila of Manikganj, after a college girl lodged a complaint alleging she had a long-term physical relationship with the expatriate on the promise of marriage......

item.description: Five postgraduate students from the Department of Chemistry at Jagannath University are set to represent Bangladesh at an international academic conference to be held at Jadavpur University in the Indian state of West Bengal......

item.description: An Israeli strike, one of multiple on the southern Lebanese city of Nabatiyeh on Friday, killed at least 10 State Security personnel, a source from the government agency said, as Lebanon prepared to start ceasefire talks with Israel......

item.description: A police complaint has been filed against Dhaka Medical College Hospital gynaecology professor Rashida Khanom who allegedly conducted a wrong operation on a patient at a private hospital in Dhaka city......

item.description: Three individuals were arrested while allegedly attempting to rob a battery-run rickshaw in the capital’s Mohakhali area on early Friday morning......

item.description: Bangladesh and Malaysia on Friday agreed to strengthen cooperation in higher education, scholarships and skills development, with Dhaka seeking expanded opportunities for students and enhanced workforce mobility......

item.description: Politicians, academics and former student leaders on Friday called on opposition parties to unite and launch a movement to compel the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to scrap what they described as an ‘unequal’ trade deal signed with the United States during the interim government’s tenure......Geographic Reference

item.description: Residents in Meherpur district alleged excessive hike in the price of liquefied petroleum gas with 12-kg cylinders selling at nearly Tk 2,000, well above the government-fixed price of Tk 1,728. .....

item.description: At least 1,177 new suspected measles cases and four more suspected measles deaths were reported in the 24 hours until 8:00am Friday, according to the Directorate General of Health Services......

item.title: Govt examines viability of repair of DEMU trains again
item.description: The government is considering repairing Diesel Electric Multiple Unit trains, as 20 train sets introduced in 2013 at a cost of about Tk 600 crore have remained inoperative for several years, although the repair of two of the trains has proven futile......Bangladesh travel

item.description: Uncertainties over availability of fuel oils are still troubling most of the consumers in the capital Dhaka and other cities in the country amid a supply shortage of petroleum products and a high demand for the items......
